title: Shrimp Halva
description: A rich and dense Syrian dish featuring dried shrimp cooked with a sweet mixture of tahini, sugar, and lemon juice for an energizing treat suitable for halal consumption.
instructions: Place the dried shrimp in a mixing bowl.
Add tahini, sugar, lemon juice, ground cumin and water to the bowl with the shrimp.
Mix all ingredients until they are well combined.
Let the mixture sit for at least 30 minutes before cooking.
Bring a saucepan of water to a boil over medium-high heat.
Gently place the mixture into the boiling water, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking and burning.
Cook the mixture for about 15-20 minutes or until it thickens and forms a smooth paste.
Remove from heat once done. Serve hot with your choice of accompaniments such as pita bread or vegetables.
